brief_title: Afatinib in EGFR+NSCLC (Recurrent or Stage IV) - Patients With Poor Performance Status (ECOG 2 or 3)
official_title: An Open-label, Single-arm Phase IV Study of Afatinib in Patients With Stage IV or Recurrent Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Who Have Poor Performance Status and Whose Tumors Have the Common Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) Mutations, Exon 19 Deletions or Exon 21(L858R) Substitution Mutations

outcomes.title: Percentage of Patients With Occurrence of Adverse Events (AEs) Leading to Dose Reduction of Afatinib
outcomes.description: Percentage of patients with occurrence of Adverse Events (AEs) leading to dose reduction of afatinib.

outcomes.title: Percentage of Patients With Occurrence of CTCAE Grade 3 or Higher Diarrhoea, Rash/Acne+, Stomatitis+ and Paronychia+ (+ Represents Grouped Term)
outcomes.description: Percentage of patients with occurrence of Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) grade 3 or higher diarrhoea, rash/acne+, stomatitis+ and paronychia+ (+ represents grouped term).

outcomes.title: Time to First Dose Reduction of Afatinib Caused by Adverse Events (AEs)
outcomes.description: Time to first dose reduction of afatinib caused by Adverse Events (AEs) defined as time from the date of the first administration of afatinib to the first dose reduction of afatinib caused by AEs.

trial_name: ECOG Phase II trial of octreotide ± prednisone in thymoma and thymic carcinoma
